body: tracker.py event_entries() calls json.loads() on each line of the .events.jsonl file with no error handling. A single corrupt or truncated line (e.g. from a crash mid-write) will raise json.JSONDecodeError and break all commands that touch that ticket's history: show, resume, history, note_count, list (via render). Should catch the error per-line and either skip with a warning or surface a clear diagnostic. Related to T-82 (validate tracker files on load) but narrower: this is a concrete crash path today.
title: event_entries() crashes on malformed JSONL lines
